titleStmt.title: Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 626

div.p: Alexander, by the grace of God king of Scots, to all worthy men of his whole land, greeting: Know that we have granted to God and to St Cuthbert at Coldingham and to the monks serving God there that they and their servants throughout our whole land be free and quit from all toll for their own chattels and merchandise. Wherefore we forbid that anyone unjustly bring any injury or violence on them contrary to this liberty within our land, upon our forfeiture, as the charter of the lord King William, our father, bears witness. As witnesses: William del Bois, chancellor; William Comyn, earl of Buchan, justiciar of the Scots; Alexander, sheriff of Stirling; John of Maxwell. At Kinross on the 21st day of July, in the eighth year of our reign.
